description: Satellite observer (ground station) geometry uses a spherical Earth instead of the WGS84 ellipsoid, so the observer's Earth-centered position is wrong away from the equator — off by about 21 km at the poles. Expected: the observer position follows WGS84 (equatorial radius a = 6378.137 km, 1/f = 298.257223563): distance from Earth's center equals a at latitude 0 and the polar radius a*(1-f) (about 6356.752 km) at latitude 90. Observer altitude in km above the ellipsoid must also be honored, raising the position along the local ellipsoid normal. Acceptance: observer position magnitude matches those radii at equator and pole, and a positive altitude increases the magnitude accordingly.
